channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Voice conversations not shown in Claude.ai sidebar
item.description: Apr 2, 23:16 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Apr 2, 20:35 UTCUpdate - Although we are observing recovery once again, we expect continued periods of degradation. Work that is queued during times of degradation does eventually get processed. We continue to investigate and find a mitigation, and will update again within 2 hours.Apr 2, 19:28...

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Services (AWS) secrets, shell command history, Stripe API keys, and GitHub tokens...

item.title: Elevated latency uploading Workers Static Assets
item.description: Apr 2, 18:47 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 2, 17:39 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating elevated latency uploading Workers Static Assets. Workers scripts which are already uploaded are not impacted.

item.title: Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak
item.description: Within days of each other, Anthropic first leaked the source code to Claude Code, and then a critical vulnerability was found by Adversa AI. The post Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Moonlake: Causal World Models should be Multimodal, Interactive, and Efficient — with Chris Manning and Fan-yun Sun
item.description: We cap out our World Models coverage with one of the most exciting new approaches - long running, multiplayer, interactive world models built with agents bootstrapped from game engines!

item.title: Digital Experience Monitoring (DEX) Delay
item.description: Apr 2, 16:50 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue where Digital Experience Monitoring (DEX) data delivered via Logpush may experience delays. DEX dashboard functionality and other Logpush datasets are not affected.

item.title: ChatGPT Dictation Elevated Error Rate
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services and working on mitigating impact.Affected components Voice mode (Partial outage)

item.title: Copilot Coding Agent failing to start some jobs
item.description: Apr 2, 16:30 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Apr 2, 16:28 UTCUpdate - When assigning tasks to Copilot Cloud Agent, the task will appear...

item.title: Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices
item.description: The DarkSword exploit kit has been used by both state-sponsored hackers and commercial spyware vendors. The post Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
item.description: Gemma 4: Our most intelligent open models to date, purpose-built for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

item.title: Cisco Patches 9.8 CVSS IMC and SSM Flaws Allowing Remote System Compromise
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and gain access to the system with elevated privileges. The vulnerability,...

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 38 Deals Announced in March 2026
item.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Airbus, Cellebrite, Databricks, Quantum eMotion, Rapid7, and OpenAI. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 38 Deals Announced in March 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cache Purging Delays
item.description: Apr 2, 12:58 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 2, 12:43 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Apr 2, 12:41 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare single-file cache purging (purge by URL) called via the Dashboard/API are delayed. We are working to resolve this issue as soon...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
item.description: The latest ThreatsDay Bulletin is basically a cheat sheet for everything breaking on the internet right now. No corporate fluff or boring lectures here, just a quick and honest look at the messy reality of keeping systems safe this week. Things are moving fast. The list...

item.title: Issues with creating Managed Rules exceptions via the Cloudflare Dashboard
item.description: Apr 2, 12:42 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with creating Managed Rules exceptions via the Cloudflare Dashboard. These issues do not affect the Cloudflare API, serving of cached files via the Cloudflare CDN or other security features at the Cloudflare Edge. Customers using...

item.title: Cisco Patches Critical and High-Severity Vulnerabilities
item.description: The bugs could lead to authentication bypass, remote code execution, information disclosure, and privilege escalation. The post Cisco Patches Critical and High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.  As the war in Iran continues, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been...

item.title: 250,000 Affected by Data Breach at Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital
item.description: In January 2026, a threat actor hacked the hospital’s internal network and stole personal and health information. The post 250,000 Affected by Data Breach at Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A financially motivated operation codenamed REF1695 has been observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November 2023. "Beyond cryptomining, the threat actor monetizes infections through CPA (Cost Per Action)...

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December 2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of container image projects, versions, images, language libraries, and builds. These insights...

item.title: Cloudflare AI Search Issues
item.description: Apr 2, 10:29 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 2, 10:17 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 2, 09:05 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with AI Search. Customers may observe 7001 and internal error...

item.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
item.description: As the war in Iran continues to engulf the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been fossil-fuel prices. In particular, you can’t get away from news about the price of gasoline, which just topped an average of $4 a gallon in...

item.title: LibTV Integrates Seedance 2.0 to Streamline AI Video Production
item.description: LibTV has integrated the Seedance 2.0 model to enhance AI video production, enabling multi-modal content creation with reduced latency of around 2–3 minutes per clip. The platform also introduces a node-based workflow and agent-driven automation for end-to-end video generation.

item.title: Heyi New Energy Secures Hundreds of Millions in Series A+ to Advance Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Heyi New Energy has raised several hundred million RMB (estimated $40–70 million USD) to expand solid-state battery production, with energy density reaching over 500 Wh/kg for lithium-metal systems.

item.title: Niuxin Semiconductor Expands PCIe IP Portfolio Across Multiple Generations
item.description: Niuxin Semiconductor has built a full PCIe IP portfolio from 3.0 to 5.0, supporting applications in AI computing and data centers, with PCIe 6.0 already developed and moving toward commercialization.

item.title: Xinghaitu Raises Nearly RMB 2 Billion, Valuation Exceeds RMB 20 Billion
item.description: Embodied AI firm Xinghaitu has raised nearly $280 million (RMB 2B), bringing its valuation above $2.8 billion (RMB 20B). The company is scaling robotics deployment while advancing vision-language-action and world models.

item.title: High error rate for completionsapi-gpt-5-nano
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Chat Completions (Degraded performance)

item.title: XREAL Files for Hong Kong IPO, Targets Smart Glasses Leadership
item.description: XREAL has filed for a Hong Kong IPO as it targets leadership in smart glasses, reporting 2025 revenue of about $72 million (RMB 516M) and over 70% overseas sales. The company is expanding into spatial computing and AI-powered devices through partnerships including Google.

item.title: [AINews] A quiet April Fools
item.description: a quiet day

item.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
item.description: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat...

item.title: One Panel Does Not Fit All: Case-Adaptive Multi-Agent Deliberation for Clinical Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.00085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models applied to clinical prediction exhibit case-level heterogeneity: simple cases yield consistent outputs, while complex cases produce divergent predictions under minor prompt changes. Existing single-agent strategies sample from...

item.title: Open, Reliable, and Collective: A Community-Driven Framework for Tool-Using AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.00137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-integrated LLMs can retrieve, compute, and take real-world actions via external tools, but reliability remains a key bottleneck. We argue that failures stem from both tool-use accuracy (how well an agent invokes a tool) and intrinsic tool...

item.title: A Safety-Aware Role-Orchestrated Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Behavioral Health Communication Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-agent large language model (LLM) systems struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions and maintain safety in behavioral health communication. We propose a safety-aware, role-orchestrated multi-agent LLM framework designed...

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop Control of Objective Drift in LLM-Assisted Computer Science Education
item.description: arXiv:2604.00281v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in computer science education through AI-assisted programming tools, yet such workflows often exhibit objective drift, in which locally plausible outputs diverge from stated task specifications....

item.title: Two-Stage Optimizer-Aware Online Data Selection for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.00001v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gradient-based data selection offers a principled framework for estimating sample utility in large language model (LLM) fine-tuning, but existing methods are mostly designed for offline settings. They are therefore less suited to online fine-tuning,...

item.title: Task-Centric Personalized Federated Fine-Tuning of Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.00050v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising technique for training language models on distributed and private datasets of diverse tasks. However, aggregating models trained on heterogeneous tasks often degrades the overall performance of...

item.title: Evolution Strategies for Deep RL pretraining
item.description: arXiv:2604.00066v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although Deep Reinforcement Learning has proven highly effective for complex decision-making problems, it demands significant computational resources and careful parameter adjustment in order to develop successful strategies. Evolution strategies...

item.title: Temporal Memory for Resource-Constrained Agents: Continual Learning via Stochastic Compress-Add-Smooth
item.description: arXiv:2604.00067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: An agent that operates sequentially must incorporate new experience without forgetting old experience, under a fixed memory budget. We propose a framework in which memory is not a parameter vector but a stochastic process: a Bridge Diffusion on a...

item.title: Perspective: Towards sustainable exploration of chemical spaces with machine learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.00069v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence is transforming molecular and materials science, but its growing computational and data demands raise critical sustainability challenges. In this Perspective, we examine resource considerations across the AI-driven discovery...

item.title: PolarQuant: Optimal Gaussian Weight Quantization via Hadamard Rotation for LLM Compression
item.description: arXiv:2603.29078v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present PolarQuant, a post-training weight quantization method for large language models (LLMs) that exploits the distributional structure of neural network weights to achieve near-lossless compression. PolarQuant operates in three stages: (1)...

item.title: APEX-EM: Non-Parametric Online Learning for Autonomous Agents via Structured Procedural-Episodic Experience Replay
item.description: arXiv:2603.29093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based autonomous agents lack persistent procedural memory: they re-derive solutions from scratch even when structurally identical tasks have been solved before. We present \textbf{APEX-EM}, a non-parametric online learning framework that...

item.title: Concept Training for Human-Aligned Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.29123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The next-token prediction (NTP) objective trains language models to predict a single continuation token at each step. In natural language, however, a prefix can be continued in many valid ways, and even similar meanings may differ in surface form. For...

item.title: Kwame 2.0: Human-in-the-Loop Generative AI Teaching Assistant for Large Scale Online Coding Education in Africa
item.description: arXiv:2603.29159v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Providing timely and accurate learning support in large-scale online coding courses is challenging, particularly in resource-constrained contexts. We present Kwame 2.0, a bilingual (English-French) generative AI teaching assistant built using...

item.title: SyriSign: A Parallel Corpus for Arabic Text to Syrian Arabic Sign Language Translation
item.description: arXiv:2603.29219v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sign language is the primary approach of communication for the Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing (DHH) community. While there are numerous benchmarks for high-resource sign languages, low-resource languages like Arabic remain underrepresented. Currently, there...

item.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
Errors on the Claude.ai desktop application
item.description: Apr 1, 22:16 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 1, 22:15 UTCIdentified - We have identified an issue which results in the Claude.ai desktop application returning an error when attempting to connect. We are working to resolve this issue as quickly as possible, and the Claude.ai...

item.title: Open-Set Supervised 3D Anomaly Detection: An Industrial Dataset and a Generalisable Framework for Unknown Defects
item.description: Although self-supervised 3D anomaly detection assumes that acquiring high-precision point clouds is computationally expensive, in real manufacturing scenarios it is often feasible to collect a limited number of anomalous samples. Therefore, we study open-set supervised 3D anomaly detection, where...

item.title: Online Reasoning Calibration: Test-Time Training Enables Generalizable Conformal LLM Reasoning
item.description: While test-time scaling has enabled large language models to solve highly difficult tasks, state-of-the-art results come at exorbitant compute costs. These inefficiencies can be attributed to the miscalibration of post-trained language models, and the lack of calibration in popular sampling...

item.title: Property-Level Flood Risk Assessment Using AI-Enabled Street-View Lowest Floor Elevation Extraction and ML Imputation Across Texas
item.description: This paper argues that AI-enabled analysis of street-view imagery, complemented by performance-gated machine-learning imputation, provides a viable pathway for generating building-specific elevation data at regional scale for flood risk assessment. We develop and apply a three-stage pipeline across...
